qa_pair.question: Use the Semantic Scholar MCP tools to resolve the DOI "10.1038/nature14539" to its canonical Semantic Scholar paperId (CorpusId). Return only the numeric CorpusId.
Look up the 2017 paper "Attention Is All You Need" on Semantic Scholar. How many authors are listed on the paper record? Return a single integer.

The paper "Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition" (He et al., 2015) has a Semantic Scholar record. In what year was it first published according to the Semantic Scholar `year` field? Return the four-digit year.

Using s2_search_authors, find the Semantic Scholar authorId for the author named "Yoshua Bengio" (the prolific deep-learning researcher, not any namesake). Return the numeric authorId string exactly as reported.

The arXiv preprint arXiv:1512.03385 corresponds to a seminal computer-vision paper. Use s2_get_paper with the id "ARXIV:1512.03385" and return the first author's family name (surname) exactly as listed.
Use s2_match_paper_by_title with the exact title "ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks". Return the publication year (four digits) reported on the matched paper.

Using s2_get_paper_references on the paper with DOI 10.1038/nature14539 ("Deep Learning", LeCun/Bengio/Hinton, 2015), how many reference entries are reported by Semantic Scholar's `referenceCount` field on that paper? Return a single integer.

Using s2_list_dataset_releases, what is the dataset release_id format used by Semantic Scholar? Answer with the literal format string (e.g. "YYYY-MM-DD") describing the pattern of the release identifiers returned.
Use s2_resolve_identifier on the PubMed ID "PMID:20813121" and return the Semantic Scholar paperId (CorpusId) exactly as reported.

The paper "Generative Adversarial Nets" (Goodfellow et al., NIPS 2014) has a Semantic Scholar record. Use s2_match_paper_by_title to find it and return the venue string exactly as Semantic Scholar reports it in the `venue` field.
